questions.q: In poetry, what is the term for a group of consecutive lines that form a unit within a poem?
Which British poet wrote 'The Tyger,' a famous poem featuring the line 'Tyger Tyger, burning bright'?
What is the name of the five-line humorous poem form that takes its name from an Irish county?
Maya Angelou wrote which famous inspirational poem with the title 'Still I ___'?
Which country is the origin of haiku, the traditional three-line poetry form?
Edgar Allan Poe's most famous poem features which bird as its central symbol?
Which English poet composed 'Ozymandias,' a sonnet about a fallen statue in the desert?
William Wordsworth wrote a famous poem about wandering lonely as which natural object?
In which century did Dante write his epic poem 'The Divine Comedy'?
What is the name of Robert Burns's poem that describes a creature as 'sleekit, cowrin', and timorous'?
The epic poem 'Beowulf' is set in which historical region of the world?
Which one-word title is the name of a famous Rudyard Kipling poem about perseverance?
In Shakespeare's Sonnet 18, which month's darling buds are mentioned?
Which South American poet, known for love poetry, was born in Chile?
What type of flowers grow in the poem 'In Flanders Fields' by John McCrae?
The musical 'Cats' was created based on poetry by which 20th-century British poet?
Which French poet, famous for 'Les Fleurs du Mal,' shares his first name with Prince Charles?
Lewis Carroll's nonsense poem 'Jabberwocky' features which made-up creature as its subject?
Wilfred Owen's poetry is primarily associated with which major historical conflict?
Sylvia Plath wrote only one novel during her lifetime. What is its title?
In W.H. Auden's 'Funeral Blues,' what is repeatedly described as stopped?
Which Romantic poet wrote 'La Belle Dame sans Merci'?
What is the name of the metrical foot consisting of one unstressed syllable followed by one stressed syllable?
In T.S. Eliot's 'The Waste Land,' which European capital city is explicitly mentioned as 'falling'?
Stephane Mallarmé's poem 'Un Coup de Dés' (A Throw of the Dice) is notable for pioneering what unconventional poetic element?
choices: Concrete poetry with spatial arrangement on the page

questions.q: Which poet wrote 'The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock'?
In Dante's 'Inferno,' which level of Hell is reserved for traitors?
What poetic term describes the continuation of a grammatical phrase across consecutive lines without a terminal punctuation mark?
Gerard Manley Hopkins coined the term 'inscape' to describe what aspect of poetic composition?
choices: The rhythmic pattern of stressed syllables
The landscape imagery within a poem
The unique inner essence or distinctive character of a thing

questions.q: In Sylvia Plath's 'Mad Girl's Love Song,' what does the narrator claim to have done?
choices: Dreamed the entire world into existence
Hidden a secret in the walls

questions.q: Which Chinese poet of the Tang Dynasty wrote 'Quiet Night Thought'?
What is the rhyme scheme of a Petrarchan sonnet's octave?
In Allen Ginsberg's 'Howl,' which city is invoked at the opening of the poem?
What formal restriction defines an acrostic poem?
choices: All lines must end with the same sound
Lines must decrease in length progressively
The first letter of each line spells out a word or phrase

questions.q: Which 19th-century American poet wrote 'I Sing the Body Electric'?
In Ezra Pound's imagist manifesto, what was the primary objective of the imagist movement?
choices: To return poetry to narrative storytelling
To present an intellectual and emotional complex instantaneously

questions.q: Which postcolonial poet wrote 'A Far Cry from Africa'?
What is the specific name for a 19-line poem consisting of two tercets followed by one quatrain, with a repeating rhyme scheme?
In Pablo Neruda's 'Twenty Love Poems and a Song of Despair,' which numbered poem begins with 'Puedo escribir los versos más tristes esta noche'?
What literary device is demonstrated when a poem's words or lines are intentionally misspelled or fragmented to suggest dialect or phonetic speech?
What is the rhyme scheme of a Shakespearean sonnet?
Emily Dickinson's poetry was first published in what century, most of it posthumously?
Which English Romantic poet died in Italy and is buried in Rome?
What is the term for a poem written as a lament for someone who has died?
Allen Ginsberg's controversial poem 'Howl' was published in which decade?
Which female poet won the Pulitzer Prize for Poetry in 1957?
In Langston Hughes' poem 'Harlem', what does he compare a deferred dream to at the beginning?
What nationality was the poet Pablo Neruda?
Which Irish poet wrote 'The Second Coming'?
In Robert Frost's 'The Road Not Taken', what does the speaker choose between?
choices: Two roads in a yellow wood

questions.q: What poetic device involves the repetition of initial consonant sounds in nearby words?
Which poem by Lord Byron tells the story of a seducer who faces supernatural punishment?
What is the name of a 14-line poem typically written in iambic pentameter?
In Geoffrey Chaucer's 'Canterbury Tales', which pilgrims tell the first completed tale?
Which poet wrote the collection 'Leaves of Grass'?
What term describes the stressed and unstressed syllable pattern in a line of verse?
Which Sylvia Plath poem uses the metaphor of a beehive to explore female confinement?
In what century was 'Paradise Lost' by John Milton first published?
